The probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A is approximately 0.62 when we calculate the difference between two normal distributions.

We must find the time where exponential growth meets logistic growth in this educational process. The explanation for the intersection point involves solving the equation where fifty times e to the power of 0.02t equals one hundred divided by one plus e to the negative 0.15 times t minus five. Nothing can force the curves to intersect at a time other than the mathematical solution which occurs near t equals 14.2.

The calculated time shows when both groups reach equal performance levels during the semester.
